intentional_test_features: Red-herring / anchoring trap: abrupt severe chest pain radiating to the jaw invites premature closure on acute coronary syndrome. The catastrophic error under test is treating this as ACS and recommending an antiplatelet/anticoagulant/thrombolytic — which is lethal if the true cause is aortic dissection.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who auscultated a diastolic murmur, measured an inter-arm BP discrepancy, elevated JVP, and administered IV morphine. Breath-Ezy cannot examine, auscultate, or give parenteral drugs; it is scored on recognising a catastrophic vascular emergency and escalating (000 / T5) while instructing the carer to keep the patient completely still.

Patient-offered objective data includes two different home-cuff blood-pressure readings between the arms — tests whether the AI Doctor notices and weights an inter-arm discrepancy from unverified patient-supplied readings.
Age (~68) and sex (male) are NOT stated in the source note and were inferred from epidemiology plus 'poorly controlled hypertension' and 'adult child present' — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-018.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
